subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Venomous vs Poisonous
description: A quick, practical guide to Venomous vs Poisonous.

hook.text: If it bites you and you die, it's venomous. If you bite it and you die, it's poisonous.

buildup.text: Venom is injected—through fangs, stingers, or spines. Poison is ingested, inhaled, or touched.

discovery.text: Snakes and spiders are venomous. Poison dart frogs and certain mushrooms are poisonous.

twist.text: Some animals are both—the blue-ringed octopus is venomous AND its flesh is poisonous.

climax.text: Knowing the difference helps responders choose the right treatment approach.

punchline.text: Venomous bites you. Poisonous gets bitten. Both can kill.

quiz.question: What is the difference between venomous and poisonous?
options: Venomous injects toxin, poisonous is ingested or touched
